reason_training_not_allowed: Round 7 is currently planning-only. D2/D3 mechanism gates and a frozen D4 smoke manifest are required before any training.

repo.default_model_name_warning: main.py defaults to clean_vib; every PANDA run must explicitly pass --model_name FTmodel.

data_policy.test_may_be_used_only_for: final confirmatory evaluation after a primary config is frozen by three-seed val

candidate_pool.required_level_for_exclusion: D4 for current training implementation; D5 for stability
D4 for current training implementation; D5 for stability

candidate_pool.claim_scope: training-time hard-region contrastive boundary learning implementation
candidate_pool.required_level_for_exclusion: D4 for current training implementation; D5 for stability
D4 for current training implementation; D5 for stability

artifact_audit_required: final logits / score / margin / per-sample CE
h_text / h_image / h_fusion / h_di / h_collab / h_final
branch disagreement and branch agreement partitions
historical per-sample loss volatility if available
artifact_audit_blocker_rule: If a required artifact is missing, mark the affected candidate Blocked or create an export gate. Missing artifacts cannot be used as method No-Go evidence.
forbidden_inputs: val/test error labels used to train risk, teacher, memory, or curriculum
post-hoc variant selection using test metrics

R7-A: risk enrichment over random, shuffled, margin-only, confidence-only, branch-disagreement-only controls
risk-weighted hard-region proxy versus random/shuffled/margin-only controls
R7-B: branch partition validity over shuffled branch identity, random branch logits, confidence-only partitions
teacher upper-bound versus vanilla KD, weighted average, ordinary stacking, random teacher, shuffled disagreement
R7-C: train-only hard-region memory purity and coverage over random, label-shuffled, class-prior memory
frozen representation upper-bound versus plain kNN, class-prior memory, random memory
R7-D: branch-correct/final-uncertain recoverable coverage over random/shuffled reliability controls
sample-level aux-weight proxy proving effects are not just static aux loss volume

d2_d3: core mechanism signal is stronger than random/shuffled/simple controls
hard-region or teacher/memory/curriculum evidence is not explainable by ordinary calibration, stacking, static aux, or same-budget controls
d4_feasible_a: primary val Macro-F1 and Accuracy are not below deterministic_train_l0
target improvement is at least +0.003 absolute on Macro-F1 and Accuracy when possible
hard-bin and weak-domain metrics are not net negative
best strong control does not match or beat the primary
d4_feasible_b: core mechanism signal exists but gain is small or control moat is incomplete
candidate may remain as diagnostic, ablation, or second-stage fallback

global_no_go_rule: D2/D3 can only close current risk definition, partition, memory construction, proxy, or frozen/offline variant
D4 is required to close current training implementation
D5 is required for stability claims
test-derived evidence cannot be used for any Go/No-Go decision
promotion_rule: Run artifact audit before any candidate-specific gate.
Finish D2/D3 for all four candidates before choosing D4 order unless a candidate is Blocked.
If a candidate is Feasible-A/B at D2/D3, mark it and continue validating the other candidates.
Only after all candidates finish D2/D3 may a D4 smoke manifest be frozen.
Only a D4 candidate that beats deterministic and strong controls may enter D5 three-seed val.
Confirmatory test remains locked until D5 freezes the primary config.

checkpoint_lifecycle.d4_if_frozen: use a candidate-specific recyclable checkpoint directory
checkpoint_lifecycle.no_go_cleanup: delete or recycle checkpoints; keep manifest, summary, flip audit, telemetry, notes, logs, and metrics
checkpoint_lifecycle.long_term_keep_allowed_only_if: candidate becomes Primary-Candidate after all active candidates are gated and deep-validated
